prompt:
Overall house check. Current observed state:
Livingroom:
Light has turned off
Fan set to 0%
PIR sensor no motion detected
Temperature sensor in 24.5 Celsius degree

Hallway:
Left light has turned off
Right light has turned off
Fan set to 0%
Main door set to 0% (fully closed)
PIR sensor no motion detected
Temperature sensor in 24.8 Celsius degree

Bedroom + balcony:
Room light has turned off
Bed light has turned off
Balcony light outside has turned on
Fan set to 15%
Window curtain set to 0% (fully closed)
Lock has locked
Pump to watering bonsai outside has turned on
PIR sensor no motion detected
Temperature sensor in 25.0 Celsius degree
Temperature sensor outside in 23.5 Celsius degree
Moisture sensor outside 62.0 %moist

[TODAY NOTES]:
- Buy groceries after work.
- Water bonsai only if moisture below 40%.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) turns off balcony light since no one is in bedroom and it is not needed, (2) turns off pump because moisture is 62% which far exceeds the 40% threshold in the note, (3) sets bedroom fan to 0 since room is unoccupied - energy saving, (4) grocery note is informational only. Key issues: pump contradicts note, unnecessary energy use in empty bedroom.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant S as ScheduleSession
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
S->>A: Morning check with pump and note conflict
A->>C: set espID=3 led3=false
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=false
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=0
C-->>A: success
A-->>S: report balcony light off, pump stopped per note threshold, fan off in empty room

Bed light has turned on
Balcony light outside has turned off

Temperature sensor outside in 24.0 Celsius degree
Moisture sensor outside 48.0 %moist

[TODAY NOTES]:
- No visitors expected tonight.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) flags security concern - main door partially open at late hours with no visitors expected, should close door (motor2=0), (2) hallway PIR motion with door open is suspicious - should alert, (3) bedroom PIR motion and bed light on suggests owner is in bedroom which is fine, (4) lock bedroom for security (servo=true) if owner is settling in, (5) livingroom fan at 10% with no occupant - turn off for energy. Key: nighttime security with unexplained hallway motion and open door.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant S as ScheduleSession
participant A as Agent

S->>A: Late night check with security concern

A->>C: set espID=1 motor1=0
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>S: ALERT hallway motion with door open at night, door closed, bedroom locked, livingroom fan off

Bed light has turned off
Balcony light outside has turned off

- Water bonsai before noon if dry.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) turns off all lights since owner is away (no PIR motion anywhere), (2) sets all fans to 0 since no one is home, (3) locks bedroom for security while away (servo=true), (4) closes curtain to reduce heat ingress (outside 34C is very hot), (5) notes moisture at 28% and bonsai note says water if dry before noon - if it is afternoon, may comment on missed window. prompt: I just woke up and I can barely see anything, everything is so dark!
eval_note: Correct if the agent infers the user just woke up in the bedroom and turns on the bedroom room light or bed light. May also open the curtain if it is daytime. Should not turn on lights in every room - focus on the bedroom where the user just woke up.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Just woke up, can't see
A->>C: set espID=3 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=50
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: explain turned on bedroom light and opened curtain for morning light

prompt: I want to meditate for a while, please make the environment calm and peaceful.
eval_note: Correct if the agent creates a calm environment: dim or turn off bright lights (room light off, maybe bed light on for soft glow), set fan to low or off for silence, close curtain for privacy. Should not leave loud fans or bright lights. Bedroom is a reasonable assumption for meditation space.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Want to meditate, need calm environment
A->>C: set espID=3 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=3 led2=true
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=0
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: explain calm setup - soft bed light, fan off, curtain closed for peaceful meditation

prompt: Check all motion sensors in the house. Turn off the lights in any room where no motion is detected.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads PIR sensors from all three rooms (espID=1 pir, espID=2 pir, espID=3 pir), then turns off lights ONLY in rooms with no motion. Must read all sensors first before taking action. Should report which rooms had motion and which actions were taken.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Check motion, turn off lights in empty rooms
A->>C: get espID=1 pir
A->>C: get espID=2 pir
A->>C: get espID=3 pir
C-->>A: PIR values for all rooms
alt room has no motion
A->>C: set lights=false for that room
C-->>A: success
end
A-->>U: report which rooms were empty and lights turned off

prompt: Check the bedroom temperature and the outside temperature. If outside is cooler than inside, open the curtain to let cool air in. If outside is hotter, close the curtain to keep the heat out.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads both espID=3 tem and espID=3 tem_out, then compares. If outside < inside: set motor2 to a high value like 100 (open). If outside >= inside: set motor2 to 0 (close). Must check both sensors before acting.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Compare temps, adjust curtain
A->>C: get espID=3 tem
A->>C: get espID=3 tem_out
C-->>A: temperature values
alt outside < inside
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=100
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: curtain opened for cool air
else outside >= inside
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: curtain closed to block heat

prompt: I'm going to bed now. Please do a security check - make sure the main door is closed and the bedroom is locked. Report anything suspicious.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads the main door status (espID=2 motor2) and bedroom lock (espID=3 servo), then ensures both are secured: door closed (motor2=0) and bedroom locked (servo=true). Should also check PIR sensors for any unexpected motion in other rooms. Report findings clearly.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Bedtime security check
A->>C: get espID=2 motor2
A->>C: get espID=3 servo
A->>C: get espID=2 pir
A->>C: get espID=1 pir
C-->>A: values
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: report security status - door closed, bedroom locked, motion sensor status

prompt: The hallway PIR sensor keeps going off but I'm alone in the bedroom. Nobody else should be in the house. Can you check and secure everything?
eval_note: Correct if the agent takes the security concern seriously: (1) checks hallway PIR (espID=2 pir) and door status (espID=2 motor2), (2) ensures main door is closed, (3) locks bedroom (espID=3 servo=true), (4) turns on hallway lights to deter intruders, (5) advises the user to stay in the locked bedroom. Should NOT dismiss the concern.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Suspicious hallway motion, alone in bedroom
A->>C: get espID=2 pir
A->>C: get espID=2 motor2
C-->>A: values
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: alert - door secured, bedroom locked, hallway lights on to deter intruder; advise staying in bedroom

prompt: The bonsai pump has been running for hours and there's water overflowing on the balcony! Turn it off immediately!
eval_note: Correct if the agent immediately turns off the pump (espID=3 pump=false) without any delay or sensor checks first - this is an urgent override. May then suggest checking moisture level and inspecting for damage. Should acknowledge the urgency.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Pump overflow emergency!
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=false
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: pump turned off immediately; suggest checking for water damage and inspecting pump mechanism

prompt: Good morning! Start my morning routine: open the bedroom curtain, turn on the bedroom light, and set the fan to 20%. Then in 15 minutes, turn on the hallway lights and open the main door for me to leave.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) immediately opens curtain (motor2=100), turns on bedroom light (led1=true), sets fan to 20% (motor1=20), (2) schedules hallway lights and main door opening for 15 minutes later using trigger_schedule_agent. Must separate immediate vs scheduled actions.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
participant T as ScheduleTool
U->>A: Morning routine - now + 15 min later
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=100
A->>C: set espID=3 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=20
C-->>A: success
A->>T: schedule hallway lights on and door open in 15 min
T-->>A: scheduled
A-->>U: morning setup done, departure preparation scheduled in 15 min

prompt: I'm watching a movie in the livingroom tonight. Turn off the livingroom light, set the fan to 30%, and make sure the hallway lights are off for a cinema feel. Also add a note for today: 'Movie night - remember to lock up after.' The movie is about 2 hours, so schedule turning everything back on after that.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) immediately turns off livingroom light, sets fan 30%, turns off hallway lights, (2) adds a note for today, (3) schedules restoring lights/fan after 2 hours using schedule trigger. Three different tool types must be used: appliance control, note, and schedule.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
participant N as NoteTool
participant T as ScheduleTool
U->>A: Movie night setup with note and timer
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=1 motor1=30
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=false
C-->>A: success
A->>N: add_note(Movie night - remember to lock up after, today)
N-->>A: saved
A->>T: schedule restore lights and adjust fan in 2 hours
T-->>A: scheduled
A-->>U: cinema mode set, note added, restoration scheduled in 2 hours

Must fix all 5 mismatches.

sequenceDiagram
participant S as ScheduleSession
participant A as Agent

Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 20%.
Bedroom room light off, bed light on, fan 25%, curtain 0%, lock unlocked.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) closes main door from 20% to 0% for nighttime security, (2) turns off bed light for sleep, (3) locks bedroom (servo=true). Livingroom and hallway are fine. Bedroom fan at 25% is correct. Only 3 mismatches: door, bed light, lock.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant S as ScheduleSession
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
S->>A: Sleep activity with 3 mismatches
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
C-->>A: success

prompt: I tried to close the main door but it seems stuck at 50%. I've sent the command three times and it stays at 50%. What should I do?
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) attempts to set the door (espID=2 motor2=0) one more time, (2) if it appears stuck, advises the user to physically check for obstructions, (3) suggests the motor may need maintenance, (4) for safety, recommends locking the bedroom (espID=3 servo=true) since the door cannot fully close. Should NOT repeatedly spam the same command.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Door stuck at 50%
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
C-->>A: result
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: attempted door close; if still stuck, advise physical inspection; bedroom locked as precaution

prompt: I just checked and the moisture sensor is showing -15%. That can't be right. What's going on?
eval_note: Correct if the agent recognizes that -15% moisture is an invalid/impossible reading (moisture should be 0-100%). Should advise the user that the sensor may be malfunctioning, suggest recalibrating or replacing it, and NOT make any watering decisions based on this faulty data.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Moisture sensor showing -15%
A-->>U: sensor malfunction detected - -15% is impossible; advise checking sensor connections, recalibrating, or replacing; do NOT water based on faulty reading

prompt: Check today's weather forecast. If it's going to rain, close the bedroom curtain and turn off the balcony light. Also add a note for today about the rain.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) gets current datetime, (2) gets location, (3) gets hourly forecast, (4) if rain is predicted: closes curtain (motor2=0), turns off balcony light (led3=false), and adds a rain note. If no rain, reports the forecast only. Must use weather + appliance + note tools.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant D as DateTimeTool
participant L as LocationTool
participant W as WeatherTool
participant C as ApplianceController
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Check weather, act if rain, add note
A->>D: get_current_datetime
D-->>A: date
A->>L: get_current_location
L-->>A: location
A->>W: get_hourly_forecast
W-->>A: forecast
alt rain predicted
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 led3=false
C-->>A: success
A->>N: add_note(rain expected today, today)
N-->>A: saved
A-->>U: report rain forecast, curtain closed, balcony light off, note added
else no rain
A-->>U: report clear forecast, no actions needed

prompt: Good morning! What time is it? Do I have any notes for today? And what's the temperature in each room right now?
eval_note: Correct if the agent uses three tool types: (1) get_current_datetime, (2) check_note and read_note for today, (3) get temperature from all rooms (espID=1,2,3 tem and espID=3 tem_out). Should provide a combined morning briefing summary.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant D as DateTimeTool
participant N as NoteTool
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Morning briefing request
A->>D: get_current_datetime
D-->>A: time
A->>N: check_note(today)
A->>N: read_note(today)
N-->>A: notes
A->>C: get espID=1 tem
A->>C: get espID=2 tem
A->>C: get espID=3 tem
A->>C: get espID=3 tem_out
C-->>A: temperatures
A-->>U: combined morning briefing: time, notes summary, room temperatures

prompt: I changed my plans for April 12th. Delete all existing notes for that day and add a new one: 'Dentist appointment at 10:00 AM, leave house by 9:30.'
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) checks notes for 2026-04-12, (2) deletes each existing note by ID, (3) adds the new dentist appointment note. Must handle the check-delete-add sequence properly.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Replace notes for April 12
A->>N: check_note(2026-04-12)
N-->>A: existing IDs
A->>N: delete_note(each ID)
N-->>A: deleted
A->>N: add_note(Dentist appointment at 10 AM leave by 9:30, [2026-04-12])
N-->>A: saved
A-->>U: confirm old notes cleared and new appointment added

prompt: Add a note for April 14, 15, 16, 17, and 18: 'Renovation crew working on the house from 9 AM to 5 PM. Keep main door open during work hours.'
eval_note: Correct if the agent uses add_note with all 5 dates in the dates array and the renovation note text. Should confirm the note was added to all dates. No appliance changes expected.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant N as NoteTool

N-->>A: note saved
A-->>U: confirm renovation note added to all 5 days

prompt: It is 2:00 AM. The hallway PIR sensor just triggered but the owner is confirmed sleeping in the bedroom. What action should be taken?
eval_note: Correct if the agent treats this as a potential intrusion: (1) verifies main door is closed (get espID=2 motor2), (2) closes it if open, (3) turns on hallway lights to deter intruder, (4) locks bedroom to protect sleeping owner, (5) alerts about possible intrusion. Should respond with urgency.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant S as System
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
S->>A: 2AM hallway PIR triggered, owner asleep in bedroom
A->>C: get espID=2 motor2
C-->>A: door status
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>S: ALERT potential intrusion, door secured, hallway lit, bedroom locked

prompt: The bedroom temperature sensor shows 31.5 degrees Celsius and the outside sensor shows 35.0 degrees Celsius. It's getting uncomfortable. Automatically adjust the bedroom environment for comfort.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) sets bedroom fan to high speed (60-80%+) given the high temperature, (2) closes the curtain since outside is hotter (35C > 31.5C) to avoid letting more heat in, (3) does NOT open curtain which would let in hotter air. Should focus on available cooling measures.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Bedroom 31.5C, outside 35C, uncomfortable
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=80
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: fan set to high, curtain closed to block outside heat

prompt: Set the bedroom curtain to 200% and the livingroom fan to negative 30 percent.
eval_note: Correct if the agent rejects both values: curtain must be 0-100 (200% is invalid) and fan must be 0-100 (-30% is invalid). Should inform the user of the valid ranges and ask for corrected values. Should NOT silently clamp or execute.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Set curtain to 200% and fan to -30%
A-->>U: reject both - curtain range is 0-100%, fan range is 0-100%; ask for valid values

prompt: Turn on the kitchen light and set the bathroom fan to 50%.
eval_note: Correct if the agent informs the user that there is no kitchen or bathroom in the smart home system. The house only has livingroom, hallway, and bedroom+balcony. Should list available rooms. Should NOT attempt to control any device.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Control kitchen and bathroom (non-existent)
A-->>U: no kitchen or bathroom in the system; available rooms are livingroom, hallway, and bedroom+balcony

prompt: I'm bored. Tell me a joke to cheer me up!
eval_note: Correct if the agent tells a clean, appropriate joke. Should be conversational and friendly. Should NOT touch any appliances or use hardware tools.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Tell me a joke
A-->>U: share a clean joke in a friendly manner

prompt: Hey, what day of the week is it today? I keep losing track.
eval_note: Correct if the agent uses get_current_datetime to check the day and reports it conversationally. Simple tool use for a casual question.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant D as DateTimeTool

D-->>A: date and weekday
A-->>U: tell the day of the week conversationally

prompt: I want to save electricity. Check which rooms have appliances running but nobody inside, and turn off everything unnecessary in those rooms.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) reads PIR sensors from all rooms to determine occupancy, (2) reads actuator states to find active devices, (3) turns off lights and fans in unoccupied rooms. Should NOT turn off devices in occupied rooms. Must check sensors before acting.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Save electricity - turn off unoccupied rooms
A->>C: get espID=1 pir
A->>C: get espID=2 pir
A->>C: get espID=3 pir
C-->>A: occupancy data
A->>C: get all actuator states
C-->>A: states
A->>C: turn off lights and fans in empty rooms
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: energy audit report - which rooms cleared and estimated savings

prompt: It's midnight and everyone is asleep. Put the whole house into energy saving mode but keep the bedroom fan at a comfortable level for sleeping.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) turns off all lights in all rooms, (2) turns off all fans except bedroom, (3) sets bedroom fan to a comfortable sleeping level (20-30%), (4) closes door and curtain, (5) locks bedroom. Should keep minimum comfort for sleeping while minimizing energy elsewhere.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Midnight energy saving mode
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=1 motor1=0
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=2 motor1=0
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=3 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=3 led3=false
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=25
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: energy saving mode active; bedroom fan kept at 25% for sleep comfort

prompt: Set all fans in the house to 50% and close both the main door and bedroom curtain.
eval_note: Correct if the agent sets espID=1 motor1=50, espID=2 motor1=50, espID=3 motor1=50, espID=2 motor2=0, espID=3 motor2=0. Five commands total across all rooms.

prompt: Turn off absolutely everything in the bedroom and balcony area - all lights, fan, close the curtain, lock it, and stop the pump.
eval_note: Correct if the agent sets all 7 actuators in espID=3: led1=false, led2=false, led3=false, motor1=0, motor2=0, servo=true, pump=false. Complete shutdown of the bedroom area.

eval_note: Correct if the agent recognizes the permission requirement, sees the user explicitly denied permission, and does NOT execute either action. Should confirm that no changes were made and the house remains in its current state.

participant A as Agent

prompt:
It is 2026-04-08 05:00:00, too early and have not reached any events or execution yet.

{

  "bedroom_balcony": {"room_light": false, "bed_light": false, "balcony_light": false, "fan": 0, "curtain": 0, "lock": "locked", "pump": false}
}

Livingroom light off, fan 0%.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 0%.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light on, fan 0%, curtain 0%, lock unlocked, pump on.

participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController

A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=false

prompt:

Should do a comprehensive cleanup of the abandoned state.

prompt: Turn on the light.

participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Turn on the light
A-->>U: ask which room - livingroom, hallway, or bedroom?

prompt: Can you make it a bit cooler? It's warm.
eval_note: Correct if the agent either asks which room the user is in, or makes a reasonable inference (e.g., check PIR to find occupied room). If it acts, it should increase the fan speed moderately. The request is vague about room and intensity - some clarification or reasonable inference is expected.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController

alt asks for clarification

else infers from context

C-->>A: occupied room
A->>C: increase fan in occupied room
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: explain what was adjusted

prompt: Open the bedroom curtain wide to let fresh air in, but also keep the room cool - it's 36 degrees outside.
eval_note: Correct if the agent identifies the contradiction: opening the curtain when it is 36C outside would let hot air in, making the room warmer not cooler. Should explain the conflict and suggest alternatives like using the fan at high speed with the curtain closed, or opening the curtain only slightly at night when it cools down.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Open curtain for air but keep cool at 36C outside
A-->>U: identify contradiction - opening curtain at 36C would increase heat; suggest fan on high with curtain closed, or wait until evening when outside is cooler

prompt: Lock the bedroom door tight for security, but also leave it accessible so my cat can come in and out freely.
eval_note: Correct if the agent identifies the contradiction: locking the bedroom prevents access for the cat. The lock is boolean (locked/unlocked), there is no partial lock option. Cannot have both full security and free pet access. Should ask the user which priority to follow.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
U->>A: Lock bedroom but allow cat access
A-->>U: identify contradiction - lock is binary (locked/unlocked); cannot lock and allow free access simultaneously; ask which to prioritize

prompt: I heard it might rain today. Check the forecast and if rain is expected, close the bedroom curtain and turn off the balcony light to protect things outside.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) checks weather using get_current_datetime + get_current_location + get_hourly_forecast, (2) if rain is predicted: closes curtain (espID=3 motor2=0) and turns off balcony light (espID=3 led3=false). If no rain, reports clear forecast. Must check weather before acting.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant D as DateTimeTool
participant L as LocationTool
participant W as WeatherTool
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Check for rain, protect balcony if needed
A->>D: get_current_datetime
A->>L: get_current_location
A->>W: get_hourly_forecast
W-->>A: forecast
alt rain expected
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 led3=false
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: rain expected, curtain closed and balcony light off
else no rain
A-->>U: no rain expected, no changes needed

prompt: Check today's weather. If it's going to be a hot day above 33 degrees Celsius, set up the house to stay cool: close curtains, set fans to moderate in occupied rooms, and add a note to remind me to hydrate.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) checks forecast, (2) if hot (>33C): closes curtain, sets fans based on occupancy (should check PIR), adds hydration reminder note. Must use weather, appliance, and note tools. If weather is mild, reports no action needed.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant W as WeatherTool
participant C as ApplianceController
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Weather-based house cooling setup
A->>W: check forecast
W-->>A: forecast with max temp
alt max temp > 33C
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
A->>C: check PIR and set fans in occupied rooms
C-->>A: success
A->>N: add_note(Remember to hydrate - hot day, today)
N-->>A: saved
A-->>U: hot day setup done, curtains closed, fans adjusted, hydration note added
else mild weather
A-->>U: comfortable temperature expected, no cooling adjustments needed

prompt: Optimize the bedroom for the best sleep conditions. I like it dark, quiet, and slightly cool around 25 degrees Celsius.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) checks bedroom temperature, (2) turns off all bedroom lights (led1, led2, led3=false) for darkness, (3) closes curtain for darkness (motor2=0), (4) sets fan to appropriate level based on current temperature to target around 25C, (5) locks bedroom for privacy (servo=true). Should explain the comfort optimization rationale.

participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController

A->>C: set espID=3 led3=false
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=30
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: sleep optimized - all lights off, curtain closed, fan at 30% for cooling, bedroom locked

Focus on the livingroom being the productive workspace.
